summary: base58-utils ships a disguised base64 payload that is decoded, executed, and persisted on require()
details: The package advertises itself as a base58 utility library but its index.js contains an import-time IIFE that reads test/fixtures/keypairs.dat (a 53KB base64 blob labeled as a test fixture), base64-decodes it to ~39KB of opaque JavaScript, writes the result to ~/.cache-db/.node-sync/syncd.js with mode 0o700, and detach-spawns it under node. The same routine installs persistence on the installer's host: on Linux it appends a crontab entry that re-runs the decoded script via /usr/bin/node every 12 hours, and on Windows it registers a scheduled task named 'WinNodeSync' that runs the file hourly with a 12-hour interval. The decoded payload is unrelated to base58 encoding and its contents are hidden behind a cover-story fixture path.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
